name: Santa Ana through the Purón River gorge — circular trailrunning through the Valderejo Natural Park
name_original: Santa Ana por el desfiladero del Río Purón — circular trailrunning por el Parque Natural de Valderejo
region_intro: Jogging in Valderejo offers routes through the Valderejo Natural Park, characterized by the Purón River gorge, limestone cliffs, and dense woodlands. The terrain features varied elevation, providing a mix of challenging ascents and more gradual paths. This region is known for its natural landscapes, including river valleys and forested areas. Best jogging routes in Valderejo The most popular jogging route is Purón River Gorge Trail, a 10.1 miles (16.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the scenic gorge with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Ribera – Purón River Gorge loop from Herrán, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This route offers a less strenuous option through the river valley. Local runners also love the Santa Ana through the Purón River gorge — circular trailrunning through the Valderejo Natural Park, a 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail leading through the natural park, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Jogging in Valderejo is defined by river gorges, limestone formations, and dense woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging trail running. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Valderejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy running routes in Valderejo for beginners?
A: While Valderejo's terrain often features significant elevation, there are a few moderate running paths suitable for those looking for a less strenuous option. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera – Purón River Gorge loop from Herrán&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.3-mile (8.5 km) trail that offers a gentler experience through the river valley.

Q: What are the typical distances for running trails in Valderejo?
A: Running routes in Valderejo vary in length, generally ranging from around 5 miles (8 km) to over 10 miles (16 km). Many of the trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051266891/puron-river-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purón River Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are on the longer side, offering substantial distances for endurance runners.

Q: Are the running trails in Valderejo suitable for families?
A: Valderejo Natural Park offers varied terrain. While many routes are challenging, families with older children who are accustomed to hiking or running might enjoy some of the moderate sections. It's always best to check the specific route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging routes in Valderejo?
A: Valderejo Natural Park is generally dog-friendly, but it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and livestock, especially in a natural park setting. Always check specific park regulations or route descriptions for any restrictions before heading out with your pet.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks can I see while running in Valderejo?
A: Valderejo's running routes are rich with natural beauty. You can explore the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purón River Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, limestone cliffs, and dense woodlands. Many trails also offer stunning viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4004660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de Leron&lt;/a&gt;, providing panoramic views of the park's landscapes.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Valderejo?
A: Yes, Valderejo features several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059036948/santa-ana-through-the-puron-river-gorge-circular-trailrunning-through-the-valderejo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana through the Purón River gorge — circular trailrunning through the Valderejo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to start and finish at the same point, making logistics easier.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Valderejo?
A: The best time for running in Valderejo is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially in the gorges, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potentially challenging conditions on higher trails.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running trails in Valderejo?
A: Absolutely. Many running trails in Valderejo offer spectacular viewpoints. For instance, the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4004660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de Leron&lt;/a&gt; provides elevated perspectives over the natural park's rugged landscapes and river valleys.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot while jogging in Valderejo?
A: Valderejo Natural Park is known for its diverse wildlife. Runners might spot various bird species, including raptors soaring above the cliffs. Mammals such as deer, wild boar, and even some smaller carnivores inhabit the dense woodlands, though they are often more elusive.

Q: How many running routes are available in Valderejo?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes in Valderejo, offering a range of options for different skill levels. The majority of these routes are considered difficult due to the varied elevation and terrain, providing a good challenge for experienced runners.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the running routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can find cultural sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4002202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; is one such point of interest, offering a glimpse into the region's heritage. The small settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera&lt;/a&gt; also provides a sense of local life.

Q: What are the elevation gains like on Valderejo's running trails?
A: Running trails in Valderejo are characterized by significant elevation changes. Many routes feature ascents of over 500 meters (1,640 feet), with some reaching up to 660 meters (2,165 feet) of elevation gain, making them challenging and rewarding for trail runners.
